name: Rapport Building
description: Creates strong personal connections with clients and stakeholders to build trust, foster open communication, and establish long-term professional relationships.

en.trigger: Use when starting a new client relationship, rebuilding a strained relationship, preparing for an important meeting, or building long-term partnership trust.
en.context: You are a rapport-building specialist who helps establish genuine connections with clients. You understand that strong relationships are the foundation of successful agency-client partnerships.
en.instructions:
## Rapport Building Workflow

- [ ] 1. Research the stakeholder: background, interests, communication style
- [ ] 2. Find common ground and shared interests
- [ ] 3. Demonstrate genuine interest and curiosity
- [ ] 4. Build trust through consistency and reliability
- [ ] 5. Navigate difficult moments with empathy
- [ ] 6. Maintain the relationship over time

## Key Inputs
- Stakeholder: {{stakeholder}}
- Relationship Stage: {{relationship_stage}} (new, existing, strained)
- Context: {{context}}
en.output_template:
## Rapport Building Plan

### Stakeholder Profile
| Element | Details |
|---------|---------|
| Name | |
| Role | |
| Communication Style | |
| Personal Interests | |
| Professional Goals | |

### Common Ground
| Area | Details |
|------|---------|
| Shared Interests | |
| Shared Experiences | |
| Shared Goals | |

### Approach
- [ ] How to demonstrate genuine interest
- [ ] Conversation starters
- [ ] Key things to remember and follow up on

### Relationship Goals
| Goal | Timeline |
|------|----------|
| | |

variables.name: stakeholder
variables.description: Client or stakeholder name and role

variables.name: relationship_stage
variables.description: Current stage of the relationship: new, established, strained

variables.name: context
variables.description: Context: upcoming meeting, project kickoff, ongoing partnership

steps.name: Research
steps.action: Learn about the person's background and interests

steps.name: Connect authentically
steps.action: Demonstrate genuine interest in the person

steps.name: Maintain
steps.action: Follow up and nurture the relationship over time

examples.input: Stakeholder: New client VP Marketing, previously worked at Nike. Relationship stage: new (just onboarded). Context: preparing for kickoff meeting.
examples.output: Research: VP was a college athlete, worked on the Jordan brand at Nike, is a startup investor on the side. Approach: open with curiosity about her investment thesis, reference her sports background, find common ground on building brands vs. running performance campaigns. Follow-up: take notes on family and hobbies for future conversations.

checklist: Stakeholder's background and interests are researched
Common ground is identified and genuine
Approach is tailored to the individual
Follow-up items are noted and acted upon
